themes.exampleQuote: One of the widest tea selections I have ever seen.

themes.phrase: third space for work and study
themes.exampleQuote: A wonderful pseudo-'third space' where you can sip tea, relax, play a board game, study, and talk to other patrons.
The ambient is so whimsy. It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded.
Staff is extremely friendly and knowledgeable about tea.
It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded.
The babaganoush is delicious! I havent tried the food here — updating to add: I have been back.

notable_quote: One of my favorite places on Earth — a wonderfully large selection of teas from around the world, served in an intimate and quiet setting.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently celebrate the depth of the tea menu, the whimsical atmosphere, and staff who can actually talk about what they're serving; the one recurring friction is that seating fills up and the ambient noise level can undercut the quiet study-session vibe the place otherwise projects.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight Pittsburgh creators filmed Dobrá Tea this quarter, generating north of 2.6 million TikTok plays — the business posted once to Instagram in the same thirty days and has no TikTok presence of its own. The city is treating this tea house as a destination worth sharing; the tea house is not capturing any of that moment. That gap sits in sharp contrast to where Dobrá already stands: review sentiment is among the strongest in the Pittsburgh Cafes family, and its visual catalog on Google is robust enough to convert a curious first-timer on sight. Family leader Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange holds the top cafe slot in large part on social momentum — the one axis where Dobrá is furthest behind. Closing that distance, not in review quality or foot traffic, is what moves this ranking.
tldr_read: Dominant review sentiment and 2.6 million TikTok plays from other people's cameras, nearly invisible on its own Instagram. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Tea Houses.
tldr_meaning: Every signal axis except Instagram momentum is at or above the family median; the gap between what the city is already filming here and what the business posts itself is the single variable separating this from a higher tier.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok footage already rolling
playbook.action: 28 creators filmed this space last quarter with 2.6M plays; a business account that reposts or responds costs nothing and captures attribution before it disappears.

playbook.headline: Match posting cadence to foot traffic reality
playbook.action: One Instagram post in 30 days against a room reviewers describe as consistently hard to get a seat in; even two posts a week would lift the 2.76% engagement rate with an audience that is clearly already there.

playbook.headline: Surface the food menu visually
playbook.action: Multiple reviewers updated their reviews specifically after discovering the food — babaganoush, sesame cookies, snack platters — none of which appear prominently in the Instagram presence; a photo series on the food case closes that loop faster than any review ask.
